cases.passage: Rahul, the HR Manager of a retail company, is responsible for hiring a team of sales representatives. He has to select candidates from a pool of 50 applicants. The selected candidates will be trained for a month before they start working. The training period will be followed by a 3-month probationary period. During this period, the sales representatives will be evaluated based on their performance. If they meet the required standards, they will be confirmed as permanent employees. Recently, the company has undergone a major restructuring, and some of the existing sales representatives have been transferred to other departments. As a result, there are vacancies in the sales team. Rahul has to decide which selection method to use: interviewing, psychometric tests, or a combination of both.

questions.q: Which of the following is a primary responsibility of HRM in staffing?
questions.explanation: HRM in staffing involves identifying the human resource requirements of the organization and planning the required staff to meet those needs.

questions.q: What is the purpose of the probationary period in staffing?
opts: (A) To provide training to new employees
(B) To evaluate the performance of new employees
questions.explanation: The probationary period is used to evaluate the performance of new employees and assess whether they meet the required standards.

questions.q: Which of the following is an example of a selection method?
questions.explanation: Selection methods are used to identify the most suitable candidates for a job.

questions.q: What is the significance of the existing staff structure in staffing?
opts: (A) To identify the human resource requirements of the organization
(B) To plan the required staff to meet those needs
(C) To evaluate the performance of new employees
questions.explanation: The existing staff structure helps in identifying the human resource requirements of the organization.

questions.q: Which of the following is a characteristic of a probationary period?
opts: (A) It is a fixed period of time
(C) It is a period of evaluation
(D) It is a period of promotion
questions.explanation: A probationary period is a fixed period of time during which the performance of new employees is evaluated.

cases.passage: Sapna, the owner of a small retail store, is looking to expand her business. She has decided to hire additional staff to meet the increased demand. However, she is faced with a challenge: finding the right candidates. She has to decide which recruitment method to use: referral, social media, or traditional advertising. She also has to consider the cost of recruitment and the time it takes to fill the vacancies. Additionally, she has to ensure that the selected candidates are a good fit for the company culture.

questions.q: What is the primary objective of recruitment in staffing?
opts: (A) To select the most qualified candidate
(B) To fill the vacant positions in the organization
(C) To provide training to new employees
questions.explanation: Recruitment is the process of finding potential candidates to fill the vacant positions in the organization.

questions.q: Which of the following is an example of a recruitment method?
questions.explanation: Recruitment methods are used to find potential candidates to fill the vacant positions in the organization.

questions.q: What is the importance of considering the company culture during the recruitment process?
opts: (A) To ensure that the selected candidates are a good fit for the company culture
(B) To reduce the cost of recruitment
(C) To speed up the recruitment process
(D) To evaluate the performance of new employees
questions.explanation: Considering the company culture during the recruitment process ensures that the selected candidates are a good fit for the organization.

questions.q: Which of the following is a consideration in the recruitment process?
opts: (B) Time it takes to fill the vacancies
questions.explanation: The cost of recruitment is an important consideration in the recruitment process.

questions.q: What is the purpose of considering the company culture in staffing?
opts: (A) To provide training to new employees
(C) To ensure that the selected candidates are a good fit for the company culture
(D) To evaluate the performance of new employees
questions.explanation: Considering the company culture in staffing ensures that the selected candidates are a good fit for the organization.
